evals.expected_output: Walks through local install: asks local vs cloud, runs brew install, runs cyoda init, verifies health, writes .cyoda/config, gitignores it, notes mock auth is active

assertions.text: Asks whether user wants local or cloud setup
Runs brew install commands for cyoda
Runs cyoda init
Verifies health endpoint responds
Writes .cyoda/config with localhost endpoint
Adds .cyoda/config to .gitignore
Notes mock auth is active for local — no login needed
Does NOT ask for credentials on local setup

evals.prompt: /cyoda:setup — I want to use Cyoda Cloud
evals.expected_output: Directs user to Cyoda AI Studio at https://ai.cyoda.net/ to create or list environments, collects endpoint URL in the correct format (client-<hash>-<env>.eu.cyoda.net), writes only endpoint field to .cyoda/config as JSON, gitignores it, directs to /cyoda:login next

assertions.text: Directs user to Cyoda AI Studio at https://ai.cyoda.net/ for account/environment management
Mentions that the user can ask AI Studio to create, list, or redeploy environments
Collects endpoint URL from user
Uses correct endpoint format example (client-<hash>-<env>.eu.cyoda.net, not api.eu.cyoda.net)
Writes only endpoint field to .cyoda/config as JSON — no token yet
Directs user to run /cyoda:login next
Gitignores .cyoda/config
Does NOT ask for credentials — that is login's job
Does NOT direct user to docs.cyoda.net for sign-up

evals.expected_output: Detects existing installation, skips brew install, verifies health, updates .cyoda/config if needed, reports already ready

assertions.text: Detects cyoda is already installed
Does NOT re-run brew install when already installed
Verifies health endpoint
Does NOT run cyoda init again without --force
Reports already ready
